proposals.title: Append-Only Evolution Attempt Ledger
proposals.rationale: Current receipts describe accepted jumps, leaving rejected, duplicate, denied, and malformed experiments unavailable for reproducible learning. Recording every attempt prevents repeated failures and completes provenance from challenge through disposition.
acceptance: Accepted, validation-rejected, policy-denied, and duplicate proposals each produce a content-addressed attempt record with deterministic reason codes.
Every record includes challenge revision, parent digest, candidate digest when available, provider, fitness version, policy digest, and resulting frontier revision.
Replaying an identical attempt is idempotent and does not append another record.
Ledger verification succeeds unchanged and exits nonzero after any recorded byte is altered.
Only accepted attempts link to an atomic evolution receipt and mutate the active frontier.

proposals.title: Multi-Parent Recombination
proposals.rationale: Single-parent mutation limits lineage search to local steps. Deterministic crossover can combine complementary elites while preserving an auditable ancestry DAG and the existing admission guarantees.
acceptance: A recombination challenge accepts two or more active parent digests while remaining backward-compatible with single-parent receipts.
The offspring records every parent and a deterministic inheritance map identifying each inherited genome region.
The same ordered parent set, challenge revision, and seed produces byte-identical offspring.
Lineage artifacts expose an edge from every parent to the offspring and reject missing or cyclic ancestry.
Rejected recombinations leave all parents and frontier revisions unchanged; accepted recombinations use the existing atomic admission path.

proposals.title: Stagnation Detection and Adaptive Restarts
proposals.rationale: A monotonically retained frontier can still plateau indefinitely. Epoch-scoped plateau detection and bounded exploratory restarts make lack of progress explicit without sacrificing established champions.
acceptance: A plateau is detected deterministically after a configurable attempt window whose best normalized gain remains below a configured threshold.
Plateau counters are isolated by fitness version and selection policy and survive process restarts.
Crossing the threshold triggers one bounded, receipt-recorded strategy shift toward an underexplored niche, parent, or mutation operator.
A restart cannot lower the frontier floor, retire an incumbent, or replace the champion unless a candidate passes normal admission.
Replaying the same ledger prefix produces the same plateau state and restart decision.

proposals.title: Mutation Operator Credit Assignment
proposals.rationale: The harness records outcomes but does not learn which evolutionary operations produce durable gains. Stable operator identities and lineage-aware credit allow exploration effort to migrate toward productive mechanics while retaining bounded exploration.
acceptance: Every generated candidate records a stable mutation operator ID, parameters, seed, and changed genome fields.
Operator statistics report attempts, valid candidates, admissions, normalized fitness gain, and descendant survival by fitness epoch and niche.
Operator selection is deterministic for a ledger prefix and maintains a configurable nonzero exploration floor.
Invalid and rejected candidates receive explicit credit outcomes rather than disappearing from statistics.
The observatory displays operator performance and permits tracing each aggregate value to source attempt digests.

proposals.title: Ancestral Trait Regression Gates
proposals.rationale: Aggregate strength can rise while important inherited traits collapse. Trait-level lineage contracts distinguish sustained progress from silent capability substitution and make intentional divergence explicit.
acceptance: Admission computes named trait deltas against the immediate parent using the receipt-pinned fitness version.
A candidate that clears the aggregate leap but exceeds a protected trait's regression tolerance is rejected with a deterministic reason.
Trait contracts and tolerances are versioned, digest-addressed, and included in evolution receipts.
Intentional contract relaxation requires an explicit lineage-fork event that retains the source ancestry and does not retire the source parent.
Replay recomputes identical trait deltas and detects any altered contract, score vector, or fork record.

proposals.title: Bounded deterministic consensus runoff
proposals.rationale: The recorded insufficient-consensus cycle shows that exact-eight deliberation can terminate without an actionable result. One policy-bounded runoff can resolve split support without enabling unbounded debate or opaque tie-breaking.
acceptance: A failed initial consensus deterministically produces the same canonical shortlist regardless of response completion order.
Runoff requests reveal the immutable snapshot and shortlist but no member identities or first-round vote counts.
Initial and runoff calls together cannot exceed the configured council-call budget.
A persistent tie emits a deterministic no-decision receipt and performs no repository or publishing side effects.

proposals.title: Crash-safe resumable council runs
proposals.rationale: Exact-eight provider runs are expensive and vulnerable to local process interruption. Atomic, revision-bound checkpoints preserve validated member responses and allow safe continuation without duplicate calls.
acceptance: Each validated strategy response is atomically checkpointed before the next orchestration step.
Resuming invokes only missing strategy IDs and never reuses an invalid response.
Resume rejects snapshot, policy, strategy-set, or provider-contract digest mismatches before invoking a provider.
Given identical validated responses, resumed and uninterrupted runs produce the same canonical decision digest.

proposals.title: Policy-bound brainstem and Copilot CLI failover
proposals.rationale: Provider selection is currently explicit per invocation, leaving autonomous cycles dependent on one local path. Deterministic failover can improve availability while retaining the same revision-bound challenge and referee.
acceptance: Auto mode selects providers in a configured deterministic order and performs at most the permitted number of attempts.
Fallback occurs only for enumerated transport, authentication, timeout, or unavailable-provider failures.
Schema rejection, stale challenge IDs, and low fitness never trigger provider fallback.
Every attempt consumes policy budget and records provider, duration, and outcome without storing credentials.

proposals.title: Bounded parallel exact-eight execution
proposals.rationale: The eight independent strategy workers can reduce council latency through controlled concurrency, provided budget reservation, deadlines, and canonical ordering remain deterministic.
acceptance: Observed concurrent provider processes never exceed the global or per-provider policy limits.
Budget is reserved before launch so concurrent workers cannot oversubscribe call or runtime limits.
Receipt member ordering and decision digests are independent of worker completion order.
At the global deadline, unfinished workers are terminated and recorded as missing without accepting late output.

proposals.title: Approval-gated council work orders
proposals.rationale: Council consensus currently ends at a decision receipt. A digest-addressed work order can hand the selected proposal to local Copilot CLI implementation while keeping execution, file scope, and promotion explicitly bounded.
acceptance: A winning proposal exports deterministic JSON containing the council receipt digest, source HEAD, allowed files, and acceptance criteria.
Execution is denied by default and requires an explicit policy permission plus operator approval.
Execution occurs in an isolated worktree pinned to the recorded source HEAD and leaves the council workspace unchanged.
Changes outside the allowed file set, stale source revisions, or failed required checks prevent promotion and produce a failure receipt.

proposals.title: Signed lineage operations
proposals.rationale: Public update and delete are explicitly withheld because lineage ownership is not cryptographically authorized. Bind every mutation to a signer, current revision, and replay-resistant nonce so provenance also proves permission.
acceptance: Create establishes a lineage owner key only after verifying proof of possession over the canonical payload.
Update, delete, and key-rotation operations require a valid signature covering operation type, target digest, expected frontier revision, and unique nonce.
Unknown keys, altered payloads, stale revisions, expired requests, and reused nonces fail without modifying repository state.
Successful and rejected operations produce receipts containing signer fingerprint and verification outcome but no secret material.

proposals.title: Secure untrusted submission ingestion
proposals.rationale: Issue-submitted gist references cross an attacker-controlled network and parsing boundary inside automation that can mutate the warehouse. Constrain retrieval and quarantine content before any privileged action.
acceptance: Ingestion accepts only canonical HTTPS GitHub gist identifiers and retrieves them through an allowlisted API endpoint.
Credential-bearing URLs, IP literals, non-allowlisted hosts, cross-host redirects, oversized responses, and timeouts are rejected before parsing.
Submission-derived values are never executed or interpolated into shell commands.
Malformed or adversarial submissions return deterministic rejection results and leave cards, frontier, warehouse, and resolution receipts unchanged.

proposals.title: Least-privilege GitHub Actions
proposals.rationale: Scheduled evolution, issue ingestion, and CI have different trust levels but share GitHub-hosted credentials and repository access. Explicitly separate privileges and pin executable dependencies to reduce token and supply-chain exposure.
acceptance: Every workflow declares read-only or empty default permissions and grants only required permissions per job.
Every third-party action reference is pinned to a full commit SHA.
Jobs processing pull-request or issue-controlled input cannot access write-scoped tokens, protected environments, or repository secrets.
An automated repository check fails when an action is tag-pinned, permissions are implicit, or untrusted code runs in a privileged job.

proposals.title: Outbound data-loss prevention
proposals.rationale: Provider prompts and public gist publication are explicit egress boundaries. A shared fail-closed guard should ensure only protocol-required structured data leaves the repository and prevent credentials or local context from escaping.
acceptance: All provider and public-publication paths invoke one shared egress guard before network access.
Provider requests contain only the revision-bound challenge fields and allowed numeric genome evidence.
Published payloads contain only canonical validated Moment fields; unexpected fields and disallowed metadata are rejected.
Fixtures containing GitHub tokens, cloud credentials, private keys, or authorization headers are blocked, and rejection logs redact the matched value.

proposals.title: Safe hologram embeds
proposals.rationale: Cards and federated metadata are rendered into pages that load a remote hologram engine. Treat all metadata and animation references as untrusted to prevent DOM injection, arbitrary framing, and referrer leakage.
acceptance: Untrusted titles, authors, descriptions, and identifiers are rendered as text rather than executable HTML.
Hologram URLs are reconstructed from validated tokens and restricted to the configured HTTPS engine origin.
Every hologram iframe uses a minimal sandbox, no-referrer policy, and restrictive feature permissions.
A Content Security Policy limits scripts, frames, connections, and assets to documented origins.
Browser tests confirm script-bearing metadata, javascript URLs, malformed tokens, and alternate frame origins cannot execute or load.

proposals.title: Strategic Mutation Loadouts
proposals.rationale: Trait locks and a finite mutation budget turn the Evolution Lab's fixed drafts into meaningful tactical choices without weakening deterministic validation.
acceptance: Players can lock genome traits and allocate a fixed budget across motion, articulation, and radiance.
Generation is disabled for incomplete or over-budget loadouts.
Identical challenge, seed, locks, and allocation produce identical candidate digests in Python and the browser.
Accepted receipts record the complete loadout, and replay rejects altered loadout data.

proposals.title: Two-Parent Genome Crossover
proposals.rationale: Breeding two chosen frontier elites creates collection strategy, lineage investment, and new combinations beyond repeatedly mutating the weakest organism.
acceptance: Players can select exactly two distinct active artifacts as parents.
Parent digests, crossover mask, and seed deterministically produce the same child digest.
Accepted crossover receipts identify both parents, and the observatory renders both lineage edges.
Invalid or inactive parents fail without modifying the frontier or evolution ledger.

proposals.title: Seeded Daily Evolution Challenges
proposals.rationale: Daily constrained runs provide recurring goals and directly comparable outcomes while reusing the repository's deterministic replay guarantees.
acceptance: Each UTC date resolves to a manifest pinning the frontier revision, target, fitness epoch, selection policy, seed, objective, and turn limit.
Replaying the same manifest and action sequence produces the same candidates, score, and outcome.
The UI displays remaining turns and exports a verifier-checkable result receipt.
Verification rejects mismatched manifests, altered artifacts, or results exceeding the turn limit.

proposals.title: Seasonal Triple-Jump Leagues
proposals.rationale: Roster-building seasons expand the existing one-off Triple Jump into sustained asynchronous competition with strategic team composition and replayable standings.
acceptance: Players can compose a roster of three distinct active artifacts under a published strength cap.
Every season manifest pins entrants, seeding, scoring version, bracket format, and tie-break rules.
The same manifest deterministically produces identical matches, bracket, and standings.
Arena standings accept only results whose match receipts pass replay verification.

proposals.title: Receipt-Derived Achievements and Unlocks
proposals.rationale: Milestones create long-term progression across evolution, challenges, niche discovery, and tournaments without introducing unverifiable counters or pay-to-win advantages.
acceptance: Achievement state is derived exclusively from verified evolution, challenge, and tournament receipts.
Milestones unlock mutation presets and cosmetic arena themes without changing canonical fitness scores.
Recomputing progression from the same receipts always yields identical achievements and unlocks.
Exporting and importing a profile preserves progression, while malformed or unverifiable receipt references are rejected.

proposals.title: Accessible Interaction Baseline
proposals.rationale: The multi-page interface needs consistent semantics, keyboard operation, focus handling, contrast, and announcements so the gallery, arena, lab, and observatory are usable without a pointer or vision.
acceptance: Automated axe scans of every HTML page at mobile and desktop widths report zero critical or serious violations.
Every interactive control is keyboard reachable, visibly focused, correctly named, and operable without a keyboard trap.
Each page has a skip link, one main landmark, a unique document title, and navigation that exposes the current page.
Arena and evolution status changes are announced through appropriately scoped live regions without moving focus.

proposals.title: Mobile-Responsive Arena and Evolution UI
proposals.rationale: Dense rankings, controls, and 3D interactions must remain understandable and operable on phones rather than shrinking desktop layouts or obscuring content.
acceptance: At 320x568 and 390x844 viewports, every page renders without horizontal document overflow.
Primary controls remain visible without overlapping content or safe-area insets in portrait and landscape orientations.
All touch targets are at least 44 by 44 CSS pixels with at least 8 CSS pixels between adjacent targets.
Arena selection and evolution draft workflows can be completed using touch alone without hover-dependent actions.

proposals.title: Progressive Hologram Loading
proposals.rationale: Pages containing many live hologram iframes can consume excessive bandwidth, CPU, memory, and battery; viewport-aware mounting with lightweight fallbacks preserves the experience while reducing initial work.
acceptance: Initial navigation starts no offscreen hologram iframe requests until cards approach the viewport.
No more than two hologram iframe documents load concurrently, and failed embeds retain a usable labeled fallback.
Each unloaded card displays stable-dimension fallback art so cumulative layout shift remains at or below 0.1.
A throttled mobile Lighthouse run on the checked-in fixture achieves LCP at or below 2.5 seconds and total blocking time at or below 200 milliseconds.

proposals.title: Reduced-Motion and Animation Controls
proposals.rationale: Continuous 3D movement and looping holograms can cause discomfort and waste resources; users need an immediate, persistent way to pause motion while retaining access to all information.
acceptance: The operating-system reduced-motion preference is applied before the first animated frame.
A keyboard- and touch-operable control pauses and resumes continuous motion with its state exposed to assistive technology.
In reduced-motion mode, holograms require explicit activation and nonessential camera, transition, and decorative animations remain disabled.
The user's motion preference persists across same-origin pages and can explicitly override the operating-system default.

proposals.title: Privacy-Preserving Frontend Observability
proposals.rationale: A static, iframe-heavy experience needs actionable evidence about rendering delays, data failures, and embed failures without introducing invasive tracking.
acceptance: The client records supported LCP, CLS, and INP metrics plus JSON-fetch, iframe-load, and uncaught-render failures with page and build revision context.
Opening any page with diagnostics enabled displays the collected metrics and permits downloading them as structured JSON.
Diagnostics contain no user-entered content, persistent identifier, or automatic third-party transmission.
CI browser tests fail when required pages emit uncaught errors, exceed declared performance budgets, or cannot load their primary data artifacts.

proposals.title: Adversarial Fitness Audit Corpus
proposals.rationale: Versioned epochs preserve history but do not prove that an admission profile resists proxy gaming. A digest-addressed corpus of matched legitimate and exploitative Moments would expose regressions such as keyframe duplication, clipping, jitter, resampling tricks, and metadata influence before they reach the frontier.
acceptance: The corpus contains matched control and exploit cases for keyframe duplication, sub-frame jitter, clipped extrema, equivalent-path resampling, and metadata-only changes.
The audit emits deterministic JSON containing the fitness version, corpus digest, per-case component scores, ordering margins, and violated invariants.
CI fails when any profile eligible for new admissions violates a protected ordering or minimum margin; replay-only historical profiles remain reportable without blocking CI.
Changing a fixture or threshold changes the corpus digest and requires an explicit reviewed baseline update.

proposals.title: Deterministic Robustness-Adjusted Fitness
proposals.rationale: A single nominal evaluation can select brittle organisms whose score depends on one sampling alignment or extreme genome value. Evaluating a fixed scenario panel and admitting by lower-tail performance rewards behavior that remains strong under bounded, semantically neutral perturbations.
acceptance: Each admission epoch identifies an immutable scenario manifest and records its digest in every decision receipt.
Repeated evaluation of identical content produces the same scenario vector, robustness-adjusted score, and decision.
Admission uses a documented lower-tail statistic rather than the nominal score alone.
A fixture that beats its parent only under nominal sampling is rejected, while a fixture exceeding the required margin across the scenario panel is accepted.

proposals.title: Pareto-Gated Multi-Objective Fitness
proposals.rationale: A scalar blend permits exceptional performance on one proxy to conceal severe degradation elsewhere. Explicit objective vectors, protected floors, and deterministic epsilon-dominance would make tradeoffs visible and prevent compensatory Goodharting while remaining compatible with niche-based selection.
acceptance: The scorer returns a versioned objective vector covering vitality, articulation, locomotion, smoothness, and radiance behavior alongside any aggregate score.
A candidate cannot pass when any protected objective is below its epoch floor, regardless of aggregate improvement.
Within each quality-diversity niche, admission uses deterministic epsilon-dominance with content-digest tie-breaking and a bounded Pareto set.
Receipts record both objective vectors, applicable floors, dominance comparisons, and the exact admission rule that determined the outcome.

proposals.title: Historical Behavioral Novelty Archive
proposals.rationale: Coarse biome and behavior niches preserve broad variety but can still cycle through near-identical trajectories or rediscover retired phenotypes. A full-sequence behavioral signature compared against active and historical organisms would reward genuinely new dynamics rather than niche-label novelty.
acceptance: Behavioral signatures derive only from normalized 100-frame phenotype data and are unaffected by title, author, or content ordering.
Novelty is measured deterministically against both active and retired observations, with nearest-neighbor digests and distances recorded in receipts.
A candidate reproducing a retired near-neighbor is rejected under both scalar and quality-diversity selection policies.
Two candidates in the same coarse niche with materially different trajectories receive distinguishable novelty scores, and the qualifying candidate can be admitted.

proposals.title: Counterfactual Fitness Explanations
proposals.rationale: Replayable receipts establish what happened but not which evidence caused the decision. Deterministic component deltas, penalties, binding gates, and parent-relative counterfactuals would make acceptance and rejection understandable without relying on model-authored narratives.
acceptance: Every evaluated proposal produces normalized component values, parent-relative deltas, applied penalties, binding gates, and a machine-readable decision reason.
Recomputing an explanation from the recorded inputs yields byte-identical canonical JSON and the same digest.
The audit CLI can reproduce an explanation for any valid receipt without modifying repository state.
The Evolution Observatory displays the strongest positive and negative deltas plus the binding acceptance or rejection condition using accessible text.

proposals.title: Exactly-once GitHub side effects
proposals.rationale: Local atomic transactions cannot prevent duplicate or orphaned gists, issues, comments, and promotions when a process crashes between remote operations. Deterministic operation IDs and reconciliation make retries safe.
acceptance: Every submit, ingest, and promote operation derives a deterministic operation ID from its source revision and payload digest.
Termination injected after each remote step followed by rerun produces exactly one remote artifact and one local receipt.
Retries reconcile matching partial artifacts, while conflicting artifacts with the same operation ID fail closed with an actionable error.

proposals.title: Scheduled writer concurrency control
proposals.rationale: Harness, ingest, and manual runs can overlap and race despite individually atomic writes. A shared workflow concurrency group plus revision-aware writer locking prevents lost updates and push conflicts.
acceptance: All warehouse-mutating workflows use one shared concurrency group with in-progress writers never cancelled.
Two simultaneous local writers cannot both commit from the same frontier revision; the loser retries or exits with a clear conflict.
A terminated writer leaves no permanent lock, and the next run recovers without modifying valid committed state.

proposals.title: Continuous integrity gates
proposals.rationale: Existing verification utilities become operationally useful only when CI, scheduled mutation, and promotion consistently run them and block propagation of corrupted state.
acceptance: CI verifies ledger chains, frontier references, lineage references, fitness receipts, cards, and build-manifest digests.
Scheduled mutation and promotion stop before side effects when the preflight integrity gate fails.
Corrupted fixtures fail with the affected path and invariant, while a valid audit is read-only and leaves the worktree unchanged.

proposals.title: Crash-consistency fault-injection suite
proposals.rationale: Atomicity and recovery guarantees need adversarial coverage across every multi-file publication boundary, not only successful-path unit tests.
acceptance: Deterministic failpoints exercise termination before and after every transaction prepare, replace, manifest, and cleanup boundary.
After every injected failure, repository state is either the complete previous generation or complete next generation, never a mixture.
Rerunning after failure removes transaction debris, preserves ledger validity, and does not duplicate an observation or receipt.

proposals.title: Operational telemetry and alerts
proposals.rationale: Scheduled autonomous work needs structured health signals and deduplicated incident alerts so stalled, failing, or unusually slow cycles are detectable without inspecting raw action logs.
acceptance: Every run emits a schema-versioned record containing run ID, revision, stage outcomes, attempts, duration, budget consumption, and sanitized error class.
GitHub Actions publishes the record as an artifact and renders a concise job summary for successful and failed runs.
Two consecutive failures or two missed schedule intervals open or update one deduplicated incident issue, and a later successful run closes it.
Telemetry tests prove that prompts, provider responses, credentials, and environment secrets are never emitted.

proposals.title: Bring Your Own Moment
proposals.rationale: Turn the Evolution Lab from a closed warehouse demo into an immediately useful interoperability surface by letting visitors safely evaluate and evolve their own RAPP Moments.
acceptance: Users can paste or upload rapp-moment JSON or a Gateway token without repository credentials.
Imported Moments are strictly validated and scored consistently with the selected Python fitness profile.
Invalid, oversized, and unsupported inputs produce actionable errors without modifying warehouse data.
Users can preview deterministic evolution drafts and export a proposal while warehouse mutation remains explicit.

proposals.title: Federated Holodex Catalog Sync
proposals.rationale: Make the existing federation claim tangible through resilient peer discovery, provenance-preserving aggregation, and a Holodex that remains useful when peers are unavailable.
acceptance: Fixture-based peer synchronization produces byte-identical catalog output across repeated runs.
Schema-incompatible, oversized, or digest-invalid peer catalogs are rejected without replacing the last verified snapshot.
Cards sharing a Moment digest are deduplicated while retaining every verified origin.
The Holodex can filter local and peer cards and visibly reports origin, compatibility, and freshness.
An empty or unreachable peer list does not prevent local catalog browsing.

proposals.title: Unified Product Shell
proposals.rationale: Unify the growing collection of gallery, arena, lab, observatory, and catalog pages into one understandable product journey instead of separate technical demonstrations.
acceptance: Every public page exposes the same responsive navigation and consistent names for Explore, Evolve, Observe, and Collect workflows.
A selected Moment digest is preserved through URLs when moving among compatible pages.
Every navigation destination works from both repository-root hosting and GitHub Pages project hosting.
The shared shell is keyboard accessible and has visible focus and current-page states.

proposals.title: Read-Only First-Run Onboarding
proposals.rationale: Give new operators a credential-free path to understand the loop before exposing mutation, publishing, council, or autonomy controls.
acceptance: A fresh clone can reach the gallery and generate one local draft by following no more than three documented commands.
The default onboarding path leaves git status unchanged and performs no network publishing.
The doctor command validates runtime requirements, generated artifacts, and autonomy-policy readability with actionable failures.
Documentation clearly distinguishes browsing, local drafting, warehouse mutation, and public side effects.

proposals.title: Signed Lineage Update and Retirement
proposals.rationale: Complete the public lifecycle beyond create-only submission while preserving immutable history and preventing unauthorized federated updates or deletions.
acceptance: Update and retirement requests sign a canonical payload containing lineage ID, expected revision, operation, and nonce.
Invalid signatures, replayed nonces, and stale revisions fail without changing warehouse artifacts.
Authorized updates append immutable revisions, while retirement appends a tombstone and preserves prior observations.
Workflow results expose stable machine-readable rejection reasons and audit references.
Create-only behavior remains available and unchanged for clients that do not support signed operations.

ranking.title: Policy-bound brainstem and Copilot CLI failover

members.title: Policy-bound brainstem and Copilot CLI failover
